summary: Docker 1.10 introduced the ability to assign specific IP addresses to containers through libnetwork, eliminating the need for hacky workarounds like pipework or custom bash scripts. This feature allows users with dedicated IP blocks to easily assign public IPs directly to containers with simple commands. Jess demonstrates the straightforward three-step process: create a network with your subnet, run a container with a specific IP, and access it publicly.
key_points: Docker 1.10 added native support for specifying container IP addresses
Previously required workarounds like pipework or custom scripts
Simple workflow: create network with subnet, run container with --ip flag
Enables direct public IP assignment to containers
money_quotes: This is so cool I can hardly stand it.
I totally use these for good and not for evil.
But to use these previously with Docker containers meant hackery with the awesome pipework. Or even worse some homegrown, Jess bash scripts.
But now MY LIFE JUST GOT SO MUCH EASIER.
key_insight: Native IP assignment in Docker 1.10 transforms a previously hacky container networking task into a simple three-command workflow.
